campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. They also discussed extending a temporary rule that was put in place urgently, and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Interim ordinance 2326 being extended (temporary rule stays in place)
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue moving forward
Grant application being submitted for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide which organizations would receive grant money from the city and how much each group would get. They also discussed rules for community events and street closures, making some clarifications to how those grants work.
keyTopics: Which groups get grant funding and how much money each receives
Competitive Event Grant Program budget for the fiscal year
Updates to Community Event and Street Closure Grant rules

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings through multiple platforms including cable, YouTube, Zoom, and phone. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to make it accessible to the community.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible via phone dial-in options
Online streaming through city website and YouTube channel

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council cancelled the zoning hearing that was originally scheduled for March 25, 2026. Residents who needed to address zoning issues will now have to wait until the next hearing on April 8, 2026.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning matters postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and discuss applications for federal community development grants that help fund housing and support programs in Sunnyvale. The commission approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to address local housing and human services needs.
keyTopics: Federal community development grant applications being reviewed for funding
Housing and support services funding decisions
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February 25, 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching several new programs. They discussed a amnesty program for business licenses, ways to encourage shopping at local stores, and financial incentives to attract and support retail shops and startups in Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help non-compliant businesses get back on track
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop local
Public-Private Partnerships for city event sponsorships
Economic incentives to attract new retail and startup businesses to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026 where they addressed several items that were postponed from previous meetings. They approved a plan to remove weeds from properties across the city as part of a county-wide weed control program, and they took public input on how the city plans to use federal housing grant money for the 2026-27 fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program enforcement for residential and commercial properties
Federal HUD housing grant funding allocation for 2026-27
Multiple agenda items postponed or carried over from prior meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two key agreements. The council approved a facility-sharing deal with the American Red Cross for emergency disaster response and authorized a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late additions to meeting agenda
